It then enters a cycle of gathering an input intended to differentiate among the paths and reassessing the paths in light of that input. If the reassessment leaves the paths weakly separated, the gather-and-reassess cycle repeats. The procedure ends either when a single path has been selected or when continuing the process is no longer justified.

difference_records.left_value: The procedure starts at an initial node (N1) that leads to one or more of three unlabeled alternative steps (N2A, N2B, N2C). All alternatives converge at a common node (N3) and proceed to a decision node (N4). At N4, three guarded outcomes exist: if a threshold is not met, control passes to N5, which re-dispatches to the N2 alternatives, forming an iteration loop; if one path is selected, control passes through N6 and then ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly. Node contents, the threshold definition, the selection mechanism among alternatives, and any iteration bound are not specified in the packet.
difference_records.right_value: Given that an issue is present, the procedure moves from start to a state in which paths are expanded; three conditions (path_a listed, path_b listed, path_c listed) each lead from expansion to an input-gathering state. Once new differentiating input is available, the procedure reassesses the paths. Reassessment has three outcomes: if a threshold is not met, the procedure enters a repeat_cycle state and, on continuation, returns to path expansion; if one path is selected, the procedure moves to a selected_terminal state and then closes to end; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly. The single cycle (expand_paths -> gather_input -> reassess_paths -> repeat_cycle -> expand_paths) has no stated bound.
